heading: Reporting related to the AUKUS partnership
label: 10413 Reporting related to the AUKUS partnership
notes: Delegation of Functions and Authorities Under Sections 1333, 1342, 1352, and 1353 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024Memorandum of President of the United States, , 89 F.R. 57337, provided:July 9, 2024Memorandum for the Secretary of State[,] the Secretary of Defense[,] the Secretary of Energy[, and] the Director of the Office of Management and BudgetBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code:section 301 of title 31. (a) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 1352(g) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024 () (the “Act”) [].SectionPublic Law 118–3122 U.S.C. 10431(g)(b) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretaries of State and Energy, the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 1352(h)(4), 1352(d)(1), and 1352(e)(2)(A) of the Act.(c) I hereby delegate the functions and authorities vested in the President by the following provisions of the Act as follows:(i) to the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretaries of Defense and Energy, as appropriate, section 1333 of the Act [];22 U.S.C. 10413(ii) to the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretaries of Defense and Energy, section 1342 of the Act [];22 U.S.C. 10422(iii) to the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, section 1352(e)(2)(B) of the Act;(iv) to the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, section 1352(e)(2)(C) of the Act;(v) to the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Secretaries of State and Energy, section 1352(i) of the Act; and(vi) to the Secretary of Defense for funds allocated to the Department of Defense account and to the Secretary of Energy for funds allocated to the Department of Energy account, in coordination with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, section 1353(d), (h), and (i) of the Act [, (h), (i)].22 U.S.C. 10432(d)2. The delegation in this memorandum shall apply to any provision of any future public law that is the same or substantially the same as the provision referenced in this memorandum.Sec.3. The Secretary of Defense is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Sec.J.R. Biden, Jr.

The delegation in this memorandum shall apply to any provision of any future public law that is the same or substantially the same as the provision referenced in this memorandum.Sec.3. The Secretary of Defense is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Sec.J.R. Biden, Jr.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the signature, conclusion, or other finalization of any non-binding instrument related to the AUKUS partnership, the President shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees the text of such instrument.

children.heading: Non-duplication of efforts; rule of construction
children.label: 2 Non-duplication of efforts; rule of construction
children.text: To the extent the text of a non-binding instrument is submitted to the appropriate congressional committees pursuant to paragraph (1), such text does not need to be submitted to Congress pursuant to , as amended by section 5947 of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023 (; ). Paragraph (1) shall not be construed to relieve the executive branch of any other requirement of , as so amended, or any other provision of law.section 112b(a)(1)(A)(ii) of title 1Public Law 117–263136 Stat. 3476section 112b of title 1

children.label: i
children.text: any annex, appendix, codicil, side agreement, side letter, or any document of similar purpose or function to the aforementioned, regardless of the title of the document, that is entered into contemporaneously and in conjunction with the non-binding instrument; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any implementing agreement or arrangement, or any document of similar purpose or function to the aforementioned, regardless of the title of the document, that is entered into contemporaneously and in conjunction with the non-binding instrument.

children.heading: Contemporaneously and in conjunction with
children.label: B Contemporaneously and in conjunction with
i
children.text: shall be construed liberally; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not be interpreted to require any action to have occurred simultaneously or on the same day.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than one year after , and biennially thereafter, the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense and other appropriate heads of agencies, shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the AUKUS partnership.December 22, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: An identification of the defensive military capability gaps and capacity shortfalls that the AUKUS partnership seeks to offset.

children.label: ii
children.text: An explanation of the total cost to the United States associated with Pillar One of the AUKUS partnership.

children.label: iii
children.text: A detailed explanation of how enhanced access to the industrial base of Australia is contributing to strengthening the United States strategic position in Asia.

children.label: iv
children.text: A detailed explanation of the military and strategic benefit provided by the improved access provided by naval bases of Australia.

children.label: v
children.text: A detailed assessment of how Australia’s sovereign conventionally armed nuclear attack submarines contribute to United States defense and deterrence objectives in the Indo-Pacific region.

children.label: I
children.text: A description of progress made by Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States to conclude an Article 14 arrangement with the International Atomic Energy Agency.

children.label: II
children.text: A description of the status of efforts of Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States to build the supporting infrastructure to base conventionally armed, nuclear-powered attack submarines.

children.label: III
children.text: Updates on the efforts by Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States to train a workforce that can build, sustain, and operate conventionally armed, nuclear-powered attack submarines.

children.label: IV
children.text: A description of progress in establishing submarine support facilities capable of hosting rotational forces in western Australia by 2027.

children.label: aa
children.text: its objectives of providing up to five Virginia Class submarines to Australia by the early to mid-2030’s; and

children.label: bb
children.text: United States submarine production requirements.

children.label: aa
children.text: Underseas capabilities.

children.label: bb
children.text: Quantum technologies.

children.label: cc
children.text: Artificial intelligence and autonomy.

children.label: dd
children.text: Advanced cyber capabilities.

children.label: ee
children.text: Hypersonic and counter-hypersonic capabilities.

children.label: ff
children.text: Electronic warfare.

children.label: gg
children.text: Innovation.

children.label: hh
children.text: Information sharing.

children.label: II
children.text: An assessment of any new lines of effort established.
